title: Pseudopogonatherum lanipes
taxonomy: Pseudopogonatherum lanipes is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Pseudopogonatherum, which is a part of the tribe Paniceae. The tribe Paniceae is a group of grasses that are often found in tropical and subtropical regions. Pseudopogonatherum lanipes is a type of wild grass that is native to Africa. The classification of Pseudopogonatherum lanipes is still being studied and updated in botanical databases.

intro: Pseudopogonatherum lanipes, commonly known as African finger grass, is a species of grass native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, with its health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which can aid in wound healing and the management of certain infections. The bioactive constituents of Pseudopogonatherum lanipes include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
